findings.evidence: PR grep: `ExchangeableSigma.lean:40 def IsFinitelySupportedPerm (π : Equiv.Perm ℕ)`, `:72 IsFinitelySupportedPerm (Equiv.swap a b)`, `:85 IsFinitelySupportedPerm (π * σ)`, `:97 IsFinitelySupportedPerm π.symm`; Mathlib already treats support of permutations generically at the set level, e.g. `Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Support.lean:221 set_support_symm_eq` and `:235 set_support_mul_subset`.
findings.fix: Define `IsFinitelySupportedPerm {ι : Type*} (π : Equiv.Perm ι) : Prop := Set.Finite {i | π i ≠ i}` and generalize the one/swap/mul/symm lemmas to `Equiv.Perm ι`; leave `eventually_eq_self` and the iff with an eventual bound as the separate `ℕ`-specific API.
findings.issue: `IsFinitelySupportedPerm` and the public lemmas `isFinitelySupportedPerm_one`, `isFinitelySupportedPerm_swap`, `.mul`, and `.symm` are stated only for `Equiv.Perm ℕ`, although finite support of a permutation is not specific to natural-number time.

summary: The exchangeable sigma statements are at the intended one-sided path-space level, but the finite-support permutation API is unnecessarily specialized to `ℕ`. Generalize that predicate and its group-closure lemmas, keeping only the eventual-bound characterization specialized to naturals.
